article.body_preview:
DHAKA, June 7, 2023 (BSS) - Federation of Bangladesh Chambers of Commerce and Industry (FBCCI) President Md Jashim Uddin today urged the government to extend policy support towards import-substitute industries in the country to lessen import pressure on foreign exchange.

The FBCCI president made the call while addressing a post-budget discussion as a special guest organized by the Economic Reporters' Forum (ERF) jointly with RAPID and Asia Foundation held at the ERF auditorium at Paltan in the

llm_connections.evidence_quote: extend policy support towards import-substitute industries in the country to lessen import pressure on foreign exchange.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The quote explicitly refers to national policy for 'import-substitute industries in the country' aimed at lessening 'import pressure on foreign exchange', addressing imports at the country-wide level.

verification.quote_best_match: extend policy support towards import-substitute industries in the country to lessen import pressure on foreign exchange.
verification.judge_reasoning: The article contains the exact sentence urging policy support for 'import-substitute industries in the country to lessen import pressure on foreign exchange,' which explicitly asserts a causal link at the national level between promoting import substitution and reducing import pressure on foreign exchange.

llm_connections.evidence_quote: the Bangladesh Bank's move only to cut imports is not a solution to reduce stress on the dollar
llm_connections.scope_justification: The sentence directly discusses 'stress on the dollar' (exchange-rate pressure) in relation to a nationwide import-cutting move, matching the feature's national exchange-rate volatility scope.

verification.quote_best_match: the Bangladesh Bank's move only to cut imports is not a solution to reduce stress on the dollar,"
verification.judge_reasoning: The exact sentence appears in the article attributing to the FBCCI president that 'the Bangladesh Bank's move only to cut imports is not a solution to reduce stress on the dollar,' which explicitly states a causal judgment (the import-cutting move will not reduce exchange-rate stress) and refers to the national-level 'stress on the dollar.'
